title: pyrola picta f. aphylla
intro: Pyrola picta f. aphylla, also known as dwarf wintergreen, is a rare and small alpine plant that belongs to the heath family (Ericaceae). It is a subspecies of Pyrola picta and is native to the mountainous regions of North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which make it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Pyrola picta f. aphylla include infusions, tinctures, and salves, and it is often used to promote digestive health, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
